revision.comment: 新しいページ：植月寺山古墳（岡山県勝央町にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 植月寺山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後方部、右奥に前方部） |別名 = 観音寺古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[勝田郡]][[勝央町]]植月東 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 35.63 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 59.27 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長91m
高さ9m（後方部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[古墳時代]]前期 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 勝央町指定史跡「植月寺山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南西 |ラベル = 植月寺山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''植月寺山古墳'''（うえつきてらやまこふん、観音寺古墳）は、[[岡山県]][[勝田郡]][[勝央町]]植月東にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。勝央町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|天台宗弘誓山観音寺}}]] 岡山県北部、滝川流域に開けた美野盆地（美野平野）の西側を画する丘陵上{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、丘陵最高所から西に下った鞍部に位置し東に盆地を望む古墳である{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在は南側に天台宗弘誓山観音寺が所在する。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方部がやや開く前方後方形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける。墳丘長は約91メートルを測り、美作地方の前方後方墳としては最大規模になる{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められるが、[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。主体部の埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また副葬品も詳らかでない。 この植月寺山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期頃の築造と推定される史跡説明板。。美野平野周辺に集中する前方後方墳5基（植月寺山古墳・西宮神社裏古墳・美野中塚古墳・美野高塚古墳・田井高塚古墳）のうちでも最も早い時期の築造とされ{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、美作地方東部では最大規模の古墳として重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は勝央町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：約91メートル * 後方部 ** 一辺：約40メートル ** 高さ：約9メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約36メートル 墳丘南側には天台宗弘誓山観音寺が所在しており、墳丘の前方部南側は削平を受けている{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周辺の地形には古墳築造による大きな改変が認められる{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。前方部がやや開く点、前方部と後方部の比高差が約4メートルと非常に大きい点で古式の前方後円墳・前方後方墳としての特徴を示す{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|右に前方部、左奥に後方部 File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む == 文化財 == === 勝央町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 植月寺山古墳 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（勝央町教育委員会、2003年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=平岡正宏|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[美和山古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うえつきてらやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:勝央町]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''植月寺山古墳'''（うえつきてらやまこふん、観音寺古墳）は、[[岡山県]][[勝田郡]][[勝央町]]植月東にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。勝央町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|天台宗弘誓山観音寺}}]] 岡山県北部、滝川流域に開けた美野盆地（美野平野）の西側を画する丘陵上{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、丘陵最高所から西に下った鞍部に位置し東に盆地を望む古墳である{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在は南側に天台宗弘誓山観音寺が所在する。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方部がやや開く前方後方形で、前方部を南西方向に向ける。墳丘長は約91メートルを測り、美作地方の前方後方墳としては最大規模になる{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められるが、[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでない{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。また副葬品も詳らかでない。 この植月寺山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期頃の築造と推定される史跡説明板。。美野平野周辺に集中する前方後方墳5基（植月寺山古墳・西宮神社裏古墳・美野中塚古墳・美野高塚古墳・田井高塚古墳）のうちでも最も早い時期の築造とされ{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、美作地方東部では最大規模の古墳として重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は勝央町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：約91メートル * 後方部 ** 一辺：約40メートル ** 高さ：約9メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約36メートル 墳丘南側には天台宗弘誓山観音寺が所在しており、墳丘の前方部南側は削平を受けている{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周辺の地形には古墳築造による大きな改変が認められる{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。前方部がやや開く点、前方部と後方部の比高差が約4メートルと非常に大きい点で古式の前方後円墳・前方後方墳としての特徴を示す{{Sfn|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|右に前方部、左奥に後方部 File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む == 文化財 == === 勝央町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 植月寺山古墳 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（勝央町教育委員会、2003年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=平岡正宏|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[美和山古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うえつきてらやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:勝央町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{Japanese kofun infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: * Side length: Approximately 40 meters * Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: * Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shōō]] [[Category:Rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds]] [[Category:Municipal designated historic sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[ja:植月寺山古墳]]

{{Japanese kofun infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ** Side length: Approximately 40 meters ** Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ** Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Japanese kofun infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}} Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Japanese kofun infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}}]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | size = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tendai|ja|天台宗}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, It measures aqqroximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Aqqroximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Aqqroximately 40 meters ''' Height: Aqqroximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Aqqroximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of aqqroximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Burial Mound, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Aqqroximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Aqqroximately 40 meters ''' Height: Aqqroximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Aqqroximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of aqqroximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003.
