revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: A11roximately 40 meters ''' Height: A11roximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: A11roximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shōō]] [[Category:Rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds]] [[Category:Municipal designated historic sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]] [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[sn:etsuki-Jiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:勝央町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: A11roximately 40 meters ''' Height: A11roximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: A11roximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]] [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[sn:etsuki-Jiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:勝央町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:岡山県内の市町村指定史跡]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = [[Uetsuki Higashi]], [[Shōō, Okayama|Shōō]], [[Katsuta District, Okayama|Katsuta District]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|35|4|35.63|N|134|7|59.27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[Shōō]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: A11roximately 40 meters ''' Height: A11roximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: A11roximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]] [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[sn:etsuki-Jiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:勝央の Municipal History]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}}

The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: A11roximately 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: A11roximately 40 meters ''' Height: A11roximately 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: A11roximately 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|qid=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 40 meters ''' Height: Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|qid=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. [[sn:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Sho-o Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298191}}

Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. [[sn:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town [[sn:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. [[commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. [[co:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shōō|Shōō]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai]] temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The surface of the mound shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (stone paving), but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun]][[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q107298191]] Wikidata name: Uetsuki Terayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Shōō|en|Shōō|WD=Q1156223|lt=Shōō}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka region}}. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298191}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Sho-o Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Shōō|en|Shōō|WD=Q1156223|lt=Shōō}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka region}}. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298191}} [[Category:History of Sho-o Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223|1=Shōō}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Shōō|en|Shōō|WD=Q1156223|lt=Shōō|1=Shōō}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai|1=Tiantai}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka region|1=Mimasaka Province}}. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:植月寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298191}} [[Category:History of Sho-o Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site infobox | name = Uetsuki Terayama Kofun | image = [[File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear section on the left, front section on the right back) | alternate_name = Kannon-ji Kofun | location = {{ill|Shoo|WD=Q1156223|1=Shōō}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07656388888889|N|134.13313055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length: 91m
Height: 9m (rear section) | burial_facility = Unknown | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Early [[Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town | tangible_cultural_property = | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} }} '''Uetsuki Terayama Kofun''' (植月寺山古墳, also known as Kannon-ji Kofun) is a {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Shōō|en|Shōō|WD=Q1156223|lt=Shōō|1=Shōō}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, Japan. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Overview == [[File:Kan-on-ji (Sho-o), sanmon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannon-ji Temple of the Tendai sect}]]] Situated on a ridge on the western edge of the Mino Basin (Mino Plain) along the Takigawa River in northern Okayama Prefecture, the tumulus occupies a saddle area descending westward from the highest point of the ridge. The basin lies to the east. Currently, the {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai|1=Tiantai}} temple Kannon-ji is located south of the mound. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound has a slightly flared front section oriented southwest. It measures approximately 91 meters in length, making it the largest rectangular keyhole-shaped mound in the {{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|WD=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka region|1=Mimasaka Province}}. The surface of the mound shows traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (stone paving), but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (terracotta figures) have been identified. The burial chamber remains unexplored, and details of grave goods are also unclear. The Uetsuki Terayama Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[Kofun period]]. Among the five rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds concentrated around the Mino Plain (Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, Nishinomiya Shrine Kofun, Mino Nakatsuka Kofun, Mino Takatsuka Kofun, and Tai Takatsuka Kofun), this mound is considered the earliest. It is regarded as one of the most significant ancient mounds in the eastern Mimasaka region due to its scale and early construction period. The tumulus is designated as a Historic Site of Shōō Town. == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: Approximately91 meters * Rear section: ''' Side length: Approximately40 meters ''' Height: Approximately9 meters * Front section: ''' Width: Approximately36 meters The south side of the mound houses Kannon-ji Temple, with the front section's southern area having been leveled. Significant modifications to the surrounding terrain were made during the mound's construction. The slightly flared front section and the considerable height difference of approximately 4 meters between the front and rear sections exhibit features typical of early keyhole-shaped mounds.Explanatory board at the historic site, installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education in 2003. File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Front section on the right, rear section in the left back File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear section File:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front section == Cultural Properties == === Shōō Town Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Uetsuki Terayama Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory board at the historic site (installed by the Shōō Town Board of Education, 2003) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Heibonsha）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Masahiro Hiraoka|year=2002|chapter=植月寺山古墳|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shu11an]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|植月寺山古墳（Zoku Kofun Daijiten）|2002}}}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} * [[Miwayama Kofun Cluster]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|植月寺山古墳|version=98551015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Uetsuki Terayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298191|co|commons:Category:Uetsuki Terayama Kofun|ja|植月寺山古墳}} [[Category:History of Sho-o Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]
